title: Fixing CSS Media Query Bugs: Incorrect Media Feature Ordering

statement.markdown:
Write a function to fix the bug in the given CSS code that uses media queries. The CSS code is supposed to apply different styles for different screen resolutions, but due to a logical error, it's not working as expected. The function should take the CSS code as input, identify the bug, and return the corrected CSS code.

The CSS code uses media queries to apply styles based on the screen resolution. The bug is that the media features are not ordered correctly, which can lead to unexpected results. For example, if the screen resolution is between 480 and 768 pixels, the styles for the `min-width: 480px` query will be applied instead of the `min-width: 768px` query.

You can assume that the input CSS code will always be a string and will always contain at least one media query.
statement.inputFormat: The CSS code as a string
statement.outputFormat: The corrected CSS code as a string
statement.notes: The corrected CSS code should apply the styles correctly based on the screen resolution.

constraints.description: The maximum number of media queries in the CSS code

examples.explanation: The corrected CSS code applies the styles correctly based on the screen resolution.
The corrected CSS code applies the styles correctly based on the screen resolution.

hints.text: The bug is due to the incorrect ordering of the media features. You need to reorder the media queries to apply the styles correctly based on the screen resolution.
You can use a loop to iterate over the media queries and reorder them based on their min-width values.

editorial.title: Fixing CSS Media Query Bugs: Incorrect Media Feature Ordering
editorial.intuition: The corrected CSS code should apply the styles correctly based on the screen resolution. You can use a loop to iterate over the media queries and reorder them based on their min-width values.
editorial.bruteForce: One way to solve this problem is to use a brute force approach and check all possible combinations of media queries. However, this approach is not efficient and may not work for large inputs.
editorial.betterSolution: A better solution is to use a loop to iterate over the media queries and reorder them based on their min-width values.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a loop to iterate over the media queries, reorder them based on their min-width values, and return the corrected CSS code.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected CSS code applies the styles correctly based on the screen resolution.
commonMistakes: Failing to reorder the media queries
